---
title: "Figure 3 02/02/2026"
output: html_notebook
---

This is an [R Markdown](http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com) Notebook. When you execute code within the notebook, the results appear beneath the code. 

Try executing this chunk by clicking the *Run* button within the chunk or by placing your cursor inside it and pressing *Cmd+Shift+Enter*. 

```{r}
library(DESeq2)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggpubr)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(phyloseq)
```

```{r}
#Load R environment with our PhyloSeq object:
ps1 = readRDS("/Preprocessing/ps1_DESeq2_02232026.RDS")
ps1_filt <- prune_samples(sample_sums(ps1) >= 8000, ps1)
sample_sums(ps1_filt)
```


```{r}
#Aggregate counts based on taxa rank (in this case Family)
ps1_df = tax_glom(ps1_filt,taxrank=rank_names(ps1_filt)[2])
ps1_df = subset_taxa(ps1_df, Phylum != "NA")
ps1_df = subset_samples(ps1_df, sample_sums(ps1_df) != 0)

#Now we want to separate based on Potato Clade (A, C, M)
ps1_df_A = subset_samples(ps1_df, Clade == 'A')
ps1_df_C = subset_samples(ps1_df, Clade == 'C')
ps1_df_NA = subset_samples(ps1_df, Clade == 'M')

#Convert Phyoseq objects into DESeq2 objects: 
dds1_A= phyloseq_to_deseq2(ps1_df_A, ~ Iteration)
dds1_C= phyloseq_to_deseq2(ps1_df_C, ~ Iteration)
dds1_NA= phyloseq_to_deseq2(ps1_df_NA, ~ Iteration)
```

#Normalization step for our samples: 

Normalization using 'esimateSizeFactor' function: Accounts for variation in total read counts between samples so that counts can be comparable across samples without being biased by sequencing depth. We specify 'type = poscounts' for 16S data. Yields relative scaling and is ideal of compositional data like 16S amplicon sequencing. 


```{r}


design(dds1_A) <- ~ 1 + Iteration
design(dds1_C) <- ~ 1 + Iteration
design(dds1_NA) <- ~ 1 + Iteration

dds2_A = estimateSizeFactors(dds1_A, type = "poscounts") 
dds2_C = estimateSizeFactors(dds1_C, type = "poscounts") 
dds2_NA = estimateSizeFactors(dds1_NA, type = "poscounts") 

#Re-rerun DESeq2 on subset data after data normalization: 
dds3_A = DESeq(dds2_A, fitType="parametric")
dds3_C = DESeq(dds2_C, fitType="parametric")
dds3_NA = DESeq(dds2_NA, fitType="parametric")
```


```{r}
#Calculating coefficient vectors for DESeq2 output for each potato Clade
mod_mat = model.matrix(design(dds3_A), colData(dds3_A))

I1 <- colMeans(mod_mat[dds3_A$Iteration == "1", ])
I3 <- colMeans(mod_mat[dds3_A$Iteration == "3", ])
I5 <- colMeans(mod_mat[dds3_A$Iteration == "5", ])

#And we can now define any contrasts we want:
##For Andigenum contrasts 
res1_I3_I1 <- results(dds3_A, contrast = I3 - I1)
res1_I5_I1 <- results(dds3_A, contrast = I5 - I1)
res1_I5_I3 <- results(dds3_A, contrast = I5 - I3)

```



```{r}
#Glue phyla column to DESeq2 object in our results dataframe based on rowname in both objects!
res_Phyla_A1 = cbind(as(res1_I3_I1, "data.frame"), as(tax_table(ps1_df_A)[rownames(res1_I3_I1), ], "matrix"))
# Add significance indicator
  res_df <- res_Phyla_A1 %>%
    mutate(Significant = ifelse(padj < 0.05, "Yes", "No"), Direction = ifelse(log2FoldChange > 0, "Increased", "Decreased")
    )
res_df1 = filter(res_df, res_df$Significant == "Yes")

```



```{r}
res_Phyla_A2 = cbind(as(res1_I5_I1, "data.frame"), as(tax_table(ps1_df_A)[rownames(res1_I5_I1), ], "matrix"))
# Add significance indicator
  res_df2 <- res_Phyla_A2 %>%
    mutate(Significant = ifelse(padj < 0.05, "Yes", "No"), Direction = ifelse(log2FoldChange > 0, "Increased", "Decreased")
    )

res_df2 = filter(res_df2, res_df2$Significant == "Yes")
```


Now we want to overlay pA_1 over pA_2 for our final figure: 

```{r}
#Prepare data from pA_1 and pA_2 
res_df1$Comparison <- "Iteration 3"
res_df2$Comparison <- "Iteration 5"

combined <- bind_rows(res_df1, res_df2)

#Only want to show statisticall significant bacterial families: 

phylum_keep <- combined %>%
  filter(Significant == "Yes") %>%
  pull(Phylum) %>%
  unique()

combined_sig <- combined %>%
  filter(Phylum %in% phylum_keep)

#Fix Family Ordering: 

phylum_order <- res_df1 %>%
  filter(Significant == "Yes") %>%
  arrange(log2FoldChange) %>%
  pull(Phylum)

combined_sig$Phylum <- factor(combined_sig$Phylum,
                              levels = phylum_order)

#Plot: 

Plot_A = ggplot(combined_sig,
       aes(x = log2FoldChange,
           y = Phylum,
           fill = Comparison)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.7),
           width = 0.6) +
  theme_classic()

Plot_A

```


***

Chiletanum Differential abundance over time: 

```{r}
#Calculating coefficient vectors for DESeq2 output for each potato Clade
mod_mat = model.matrix(design(dds3_C), colData(dds3_C))

I1 <- colMeans(mod_mat[dds3_C$Iteration == "1", ])
I3 <- colMeans(mod_mat[dds3_C$Iteration == "3", ])
I5 <- colMeans(mod_mat[dds3_C$Iteration == "5", ])

#And we can now define any contrasts we want:

##For Chiletanum contrasts 
res1_I3_I1 <- results(dds3_C, contrast = I3 - I1)
res1_I5_I1 <- results(dds3_C, contrast = I5 - I1)
res1_I5_I3 <- results(dds3_C, contrast = I5 - I3)

```

Plot contrast Chiletanum Iteration 3 vs Iteration 1
```{r}
res_Phyla = cbind(as(res1_I3_I1, "data.frame"), as(tax_table(ps1_df_C)[rownames(res1_I3_I1), ], "matrix"))
# Add significance indicator
  res_df <- res_Phyla %>%
    mutate(Significant = ifelse(padj < 0.05, "Yes", "No"), Direction = ifelse(log2FoldChange > 0, "Increased", "Decreased")
    )
res_df1 = filter(res_df, res_df$Significant == "Yes")
```


***


Plot contrast of Chiletanum Iteration 5 vs Iteration 1: 
```{r}
res_Phyla = cbind(as(res1_I5_I1, "data.frame"), as(tax_table(ps1_df_C)[rownames(res1_I5_I1), ], "matrix"))
# Add significance indicator
  res_df2 <- res_Phyla %>%
    mutate(Significant = ifelse(padj < 0.05, "Yes", "No"), Direction = ifelse(log2FoldChange > 0, "Increased", "Decreased")
    )

res_df2 = filter(res_df2, res_df2$Significant == "Yes")
```


***
Plot Grid for Chiletanum:

```{r}

#Prepare data from pC_1 and pC_2 
res_df1$Comparison <- "Iteration 3"
res_df2$Comparison <- "Iteration 5"

combined <- bind_rows(res_df1, res_df2)

#Only want to show statisticall significant bacterial families: 

Phylum_keep <- combined %>%
  filter(Significant == "Yes") %>%
  pull(Phylum) %>%
  unique()

combined_sig <- combined %>%
  filter(Phylum %in% Phylum_keep)

#Fix Phylum Ordering: 

phylum_order <- res_df1 %>%
  filter(Significant == "Yes") %>%
  arrange(log2FoldChange) %>%
  pull(Phylum)

combined_sig$Phylum <- factor(combined_sig$Phylum,
                              levels = phylum_order)

#Plot: 

Plot_C = ggplot(combined_sig,
       aes(x = log2FoldChange,
           y = Phylum,
           fill = Comparison)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.7),
           width = 0.6) +
  theme_classic()

Plot_C
```

***
Contrast Matrix for North America across iteration 1 to iteration 5

```{r}
#Calculating coefficient vectors for DESeq2 output for each potato Clade
mod_mat = model.matrix(design(dds3_NA), colData(dds3_NA))

I1 <- colMeans(mod_mat[dds3_NA$Iteration == "1", ])
I3 <- colMeans(mod_mat[dds3_NA$Iteration == "3", ])
I5 <- colMeans(mod_mat[dds3_NA$Iteration == "5", ])

#And we can now define any contrasts we want:

##For Chiletanum contrasts 
res1_I3_I1 <- results(dds3_NA, contrast = I3 - I1)
res1_I5_I1 <- results(dds3_NA, contrast = I5 - I1)
res1_I5_I3 <- results(dds3_NA, contrast = I5 - I3)
```

Plot Contrast of N. American Iteration 3 vs Iteration 1
```{r}
res_Phyla = cbind(as(res1_I3_I1, "data.frame"), as(tax_table(ps1_df_NA)[rownames(res1_I3_I1), ], "matrix"))
# Add significance indicator
  res_df <- res_Phyla %>%
    mutate(Significant = ifelse(padj < 0.05, "Yes", "No"), Direction = ifelse(log2FoldChange > 0, "Increased", "Decreased")
    )
  
res_df1 = filter(res_df, res_df$Significant == "Yes")
```



```{r}
res_Phyla = cbind(as(res1_I5_I1, "data.frame"), as(tax_table(ps1_df_NA)[rownames(res1_I5_I1), ], "matrix"))
# Add significance indicator
  res_df2 <- res_Phyla %>%
    mutate(Significant = ifelse(padj < 0.05, "Yes", "No"), Direction = ifelse(log2FoldChange > 0, "Increased", "Decreased")
    )

res_df2 = filter(res_df2, res_df2$Significant == "Yes")
```



```{r}
#Prepare data from pNA_1 and pNA_2 
res_df1$Comparison <- "Iteration 3"
res_df2$Comparison <- "Iteration 5"

combined <- bind_rows(res_df1, res_df2)

#Only want to show statisticall significant bacterial families: 

phylum_keep <- combined %>%
  filter(Significant == "Yes") %>%
  pull(Phylum) %>%
  unique()

combined_sig <- combined %>%
  filter(Phylum %in% phylum_keep)

#Fix Family Ordering: 

phylum_order <- res_df1 %>%
  filter(Significant == "Yes") %>%
  arrange(log2FoldChange) %>%
  pull(Phylum)

combined_sig$Phylum <- factor(combined_sig$Phylum,
                              levels = phylum_order)

#Plot: 

Plot_NA = ggplot(combined_sig,
       aes(x = log2FoldChange,
           y = Phylum,
           fill = Comparison)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.7),
           width = 0.6) +
  theme_classic()

Plot_NA
```

```{r}
library(cowplot)

Plot_A_modified = Plot_A + 
  theme(legend.position = "none")+ 
        labs(title = "Andigenum")

Plot_C_modified <- Plot_C + 
  theme(legend.position = "none")+ 
        labs(title = "Chiletanum")

Plot_NA_modified <- Plot_NA + 
  labs(title = "N. American")


# Align plots vertically with same width
aligned_plots <- align_plots(Plot_A_modified,Plot_C_modified,Plot_NA_modified, align = "v", axis = "l", greedy = F)

# Plot grid with equal widths
plot_Fig3 = plot_grid(plotlist = aligned_plots, 
          ncol = 3, 
          nrow = 1,
          align = "h",
          rel_heights = c(1, 1, 1))  # Adjust based on number of plots
plot_Fig3

#ggsave("plot_Fig3.tiff", plot = plot_Fig3, width = 14, height = 5, units = "in", dpi = 300)
```
